TrendForce DRAM Price Tracker — March 2026

1. DDR5 Server DRAM contract price increased 8-10% QoQ in Q1 2026, driven by AI server demand. Samsung and SK Hynix are prioritizing HBM production, tightening mainstream DDR5 supply.

2. DDR4 commodity DRAM continues its decline, with Q1 2026 contract prices falling 5-7% QoQ. CXMT (ChangXin Memory Technologies) has ramped DDR4 production to approximately 100K wafers/month, adding significant supply pressure.

3. HBM3E pricing remains firm at $25-30 per GB, representing a 5-6x premium over standard DDR5. SK Hynix commands 55% market share, followed by Samsung at 35% and Micron at 10%.

4. LPDDR5X mobile DRAM pricing stabilized as Apple iPhone 17 demand begins to ramp. MediaTek Dimensity 9400 adoption is driving LPDDR5X demand in Android flagships.

5. NAND flash prices rebounded 3-5% QoQ after 6 consecutive quarters of decline. 232-layer and 300+ layer QLC adoption in enterprise SSDs is gaining traction.

6. TrendForce forecasts total DRAM industry revenue to reach $115B in 2026, up 28% YoY, with HBM accounting for $32B (28% of total DRAM revenue).

7. Chinese DRAM capacity is projected to reach 15% of global supply by end of 2026, up from 8% in 2025, primarily driven by CXMT and JHICC expansion.

Data Date: 2026 Q1
